name: Login and scopes forms in Hono JSX with CSRF
outcome: The login and scopes screens render via Hono JSX, the login POST authenticates and sets the jid cookie then redirects to authorize, and form POSTs are protected by origin-based CSRF.
scope: JSX views, login/scopes routes, hono/csrf; remove nunjucks views and lib/csrf.ts.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/login renders the login form (Hono JSX) carrying the original authorize query params
POST /api/login with valid credentials sets an httpOnly + secure + sameSite=Strict jid cookie and 302-redirects to /api/oauth2/authorize with the query string
Invalid credentials return 401; cross-origin form POSTs are rejected by hono/csrf
steps: src/views/Layout.tsx, Login.tsx, Scopes.tsx — port the nunjucks markup and inline styles to Hono JSX components
routes — GET /api/login and GET /api/scopes: validateAuthorizationRequest, return c.html(<Login .../>) / c.html(<Scopes .../>) with the query params

routes — POST /api/login: @hono/zod-validator on { email, password } -> find user (case-insensitive) -> verifyPasswordOrThrow (bcryptjs) -> update lastLoginAt/lastLoginIP -> jwt.sign -> setCookie('jid', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'Strict', expires }) -> 302 redirect to /api/oauth2/authorize with the query
routes — POST /api/scopes: port the existing pass-through redirect to /api/oauth2/authorize
src/index.ts — apply hono/csrf() to the form POST routes; delete views/ (nunjucks) and src/lib/csrf.ts
Route /api/login — verify in a browser that the form renders and a valid login redirects through authorize to the client callback
notes: lib/password.ts (bcryptjs) is kept. The scopes screen is effectively vestigial (isAuthorizationApproved is hardcoded true) but is ported for parity. CSRF moves from the csrf-csrf hidden _csrf field to hono/csrf origin checks.
